page: Microsoft Azure D2ns_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dnv6 server family. Powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with an x86_64 architecture, this instance provides 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading running at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The CPU features a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark results indicate a divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng, is average at 1665.09, and single-threaded gzip compression achieves an average throughput of 26.86 GB/sec. However, multi-core performance is poor, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 1706.89 and a weak PassMark CPU score of 3292.47. Memory performance is also constrained; large-block read and write bandwidths are poor at 17.21 GB/sec and 12.81 GB/sec respectively, and the PassMark memory score is weak. Redis SET operations reach 2.44 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is weak at 3.78 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D2ns_v6 offers a low-cost entry point for workloads requiring dedicated Intel Xeon compute resources without the overhead of local storage. The inclusion of advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX provides hardware-accelerated execution for specific vector and matrix operations. Given its network-optimized designation and premium storage compatibility, this instance is suited for lightweight network appliances, basic web serving, development environments, and entry-level database workloads that rely on external premium storage rather than high-speed local disks.
description: Microsoft Azure D2ns_v6 is a network-optimized, general-purpose virtual machine within the Dnv6 family. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 2 vCPUs, 1 physical core, and a 4.2 GHz clock speed, paired with 8.0 GB of system memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports premium external storage. Performance benchmarks show average single-core capabilities, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1665.09 and gzip compression of 26.86 GB/sec, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are weak. Supported by advanced CPU flags like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, the D2ns_v6 is qualitatively cost-efficient for entry-level workloads. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, network appliances, and basic database tasks utilizing external storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2ns_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8573C processor, 8.0 GB of RAM, and premium storage capability for entry-level workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2ns_v6 is a network-optimized VM with 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, built for light workloads.
tagline: A network-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon compute and balanced memory for entry-level workloads and network appliances.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory with a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Network-optimized architecture with premium external storage capability
Hardware acceleration via Intel AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX)
Best suited for lightweight web serving, network appliances, and basic databases
